if we want to return any error msg we can use useformState it has 2 arguments

const [state,formdata]=useformstate(sharemeal,{msg:"null"})

//here sharemeal is a function which takes formdata and validates in  server side

now we can use like this

<form action={sharemeal}>


nextjs will have aggressive caching  means

when we run production server then if we share any recipe using sharemeal
then it will not getting updated instantly
by reloading the entire page it will be updated with our new recipe

problem is aggressive caching means at starting stage the meals data is pregenerated it is stored in cache
so it is not updating

solution
using revalidatePath(/meals)----only meals component will revalidate

revalidatePath(/meals,'layout')----it means entire meals sibling and entire its children componenets al=re also revalidated

In Next.js

By default, components are server components in Next.js (when using the app/ directory).
That means they are executed on the server and the final HTML is sent to the browser.

If you want a component to run in the browser (for interactivity — like useState, useEffect, event handlers, etc.), you must mark it as:   "use client"




const id=params.id--->id is [slug]

next js

It caches even more aggressively  for ex
if u visited one page it caches all the contents of the page and then if u go to another page and came to previous page then all the data will appear without any delay .
if u leave or reload the page then only the page will be recreated
